items.question: Define a convex function and state one key optimization guarantee.
items.answer: f is convex if f(t x + (1-t) y) <= t f(x) + (1-t) f(y) for t in [0,1]. For convex f over a convex domain, every local minimum is a global minimum.

items.question: How does the Hessian tell you whether a smooth function is convex?
items.answer: If the Hessian is positive semidefinite (all eigenvalues >= 0) everywhere on the domain, f is convex. A single direction with negative curvature violates convexity.

items.question: What is a saddle point and how is it detected at a critical point?
items.answer: A saddle has gradient zero but is neither a local min nor max; at a critical point, an indefinite Hessian (mixed-sign eigenvalues) indicates a saddle.

items.question: Why is training a deep neural network harder to guarantee than logistic regression?
items.answer: Logistic regression loss is convex in weights, so gradient descent finds the global optimum. Deep nets compose nonlinear activations, making the loss non-convex with many local minima and saddles—no general guarantee which basin SGD finds.
